text: <par>Assignment of second syllable to etymon 3398 *ki CHEST is somewhat problematic, as the tense vowel suggests an original stopped syllable.</par>
<par>or 1390,1390? or 1390,1387? or even perh. M1=1382? COMPARE for earlier!! called <latinform>tɕʰi</latinform> = 1390 (e.g., p. 21)--but cf. the alternatives below p. 23 Dulong, etc.</par>
<par>Second syllable <latinform>tsa̱³³</latinform> cognate to second syllable in Kahtu (rn 137585) and Pijo (rn 137586) forms “sleep” .</par>
<par>1st morpheme <latinform>sa³¹</latinform> means ‘flesh’ , which also appears in the words ‘skin’ , ‘bone’ , ‘pus’ , and ‘sinew’ .</par>
<par>BP pfx? or reduced from prev.? M2: 315 or 2102?</par>
<par>= sha: m or pfx? (prob. not just BP pfx)</par>
<par>Borrowing from Chinese, cf. Chinese 洋芋	yángyù 'potato'.</par>
<par>Loan from Chinese, cf. Chinese làzǐ 辣子 'hot pepper'.</par>
